commit.message:
Add demo data functionality for testing UI without backend

Created demo data system to populate shopping list UI with sample data:

DemoDataHelper:
- Creates "Green Curry Shrimp" shopping list (matching Isabelle's story)
- 4 servings with 9 items across 3 categories
- Realistic amounts with fractions (2 lbs, 4 tbsp, 2 cups, etc.)
- Includes notes (14 oz, for serving)
- Recommended brand on curry paste (Mae Ploy)
- Categories: Proteins, Produce, Pantry
- clearDemoData() method to reset
- hasDemoData check to avoid duplicates

EmptyStateView:
- Friendly empty state UI with icon and message
- "Load Demo Data" button (dark teal, matches design)
- Helper text shows what demo data includes
- Handwriting font matching legal pad aesthetic

ShoppingListView Updates:
- Shows EmptyStateView when no data exists
- Conditional rendering: empty state vs. shopping list
- Added loadDemoData() method to ViewModel
- Triggers data load and UI refresh

Demo Shopping List Contents:
- Proteins: 2 lbs large shrimp
- Produce: 2 red bell peppers, fresh Thai basil
- Pantry: 2 cans coconut milk (14 oz), 2 cups bamboo shoots,
  4 tbsp curry paste (Mae Ploy), 4 tbsp fish sauce,
  2 tbsp coconut sugar, jasmine rice

This allows testing:
- Full shopping list UI on physical device
- Category grouping and organization
- Checkbox interaction
- Fraction formatting (lbs, tbsp, cups)
- Notes display
- Item sorting
- Legal pad visual design
- Without needing backend or Supabase data

User can now:
1. Launch app on iPhone
2. See empty state with "Load Demo Data" button
3. Tap button → Shopping list appears instantly
4. See all 9 items organized by category
5. Tap checkboxes to check off items
6. Experience full legal pad UI

Perfect for demonstrating the app to stakeholders or testing UI/UX
before backend integration is complete.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reorganize documentation: extract design principles to separate file (#1)

* Reorganize docs into docs/ folder and extract design principles

- Move all non-README docs into docs/ subfolder
- Extract guiding principles from README into docs/design-principles.md
- Update README documentation table to point to new paths

https://claude.ai/code/session_017FtL8evvvgsqPEXeqmN9Uj

* Trim Core Design Principles to just Player is God

The other four bullets either fall out of the guiding principles already
(open-ended interventions, no hardcoded types) or are restatements of
omniscience (agents cannot lie). Conflicting prayers is a mechanic, not
a principle — moved it to player-intervention.md.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017FtL8evvvgsqPEXeqmN9Uj

* Surface two more core design principles in README

Both are foundational enough to reframe expectations before reading
anything else, and neither was stated at the top level:
- LLMs don't drive the simulation (architecture.md calls this "the key insight")
- Time is narrative, not fixed (variable tick duration via interestingness signal)

https://claude.ai/code/session_017FtL8evvvgsqPEXeqmN9Uj

* Add CLAUDE.md pointing to README and design principles

https://claude.ai/code/session_017FtL8evvvgsqPEXeqmN9Uj

* Remove LLMs principle from README — it's an architectural detail, not a reframing principle

https://claude.ai/code/session_017FtL8evvvgsqPEXeqmN9Uj

* Consolidate all principles into design-principles.md, single source of truth

Moves Player is God and Time is narrative from README into numbered
principles (1 and 2), renumbers the rest. README and CLAUDE.md now
point to one document instead of two.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017FtL8evvvgsqPEXeqmN9Uj

commit.message:
fix: deliver feature-conditional cluster events to client (#3358)

* fix: deliver feature-conditional cluster events to client

Three bugs prevented feature-conditional events (e.g. Switch initialPress,
shortRelease, multiPressComplete) from being delivered to the client even
though they were received on the wire.

## PeerBehavior: compose all events regardless of detected features

generateDiscoveredType() called ClusterComposer.compose(detectedFeatures),
so events guarded by a feature not listed in the device's featureMap were
absent from the discovered cluster type.  Unlike attributes and commands
(reported via attributeList / acceptedCommandList), there is no EventList
global attribute to discover which events a device supports.  The fix
composes with ALL feature names so every event is present, then restores
supportedFeatures to accurately reflect what the device declared.

## ClientEventEmitter: move log inside guard, warn on unknown events

The 'Received event' log was emitted before the 'if (event)' guard, so
events that had no observable (because the cluster type was incomplete)
were logged as received but silently dropped.  The log is now inside the
guard, and an explicit logger.warn is emitted for truly unknown events.

## EncodedBitmap: fix &= → |= and bigint mask off-by-one

All three bit-accumulation lines used &= instead of |=.  Because the
initial value is 0n, 0n & anything = 0n, so EncodedBitmap always returned
0 for any DecodedBitmap input.  This caused all clusters of the same type
to share one cache fingerprint in PeerBehavior.discoveredCache regardless
of their actual features.  Also fixes a bigint mask off-by-one
(2n ** BigInt(max - min - 1) → 2n ** BigInt(max - min)).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

EncodedBitmap:
- Handle single-value constraints (constraint.value) as bit positions, in
  addition to range constraints (constraint.min/max).  Real featureMap fields
  use e.g. constraint: '0' which sets constraint.value but not min/max, so
  the function was silently skipping every field and always returning 0 for
  any DecodedBitmap featureMap input.
- Fix bigint mask off-by-one: (2n**BigInt(max-min)-1n)<<BigInt(min), consistent
  with the number case 2**(max-min)-1.
- Add EncodedBitmapTest covering passthrough, single-bit flags, and the
  FeatureMap title-keyed encoding path.

PeerBehavior:
- Restore original feature composition (detected features only) and the
  deleted comment about not validating feature combinations.
- Copy events from the full-feature cluster as a separate step instead of
  composing with all features and overriding supportedFeatures — avoids
  side-effects on attributes, commands and other feature-driven logic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* formatting

* Use Complete cluster events directly instead of composing all features

Composing with all feature names can fail because illegal feature
combinations may exclude events or cause compose() to throw.  Since
ClusterRegistry registers the Complete cluster variant (which already
has all events flattened with conditional markers), we can just copy
events from baseType.cluster directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(model): add multi-bit numeric and bigint coverage for EncodedBitmap (#3361)

* Initial plan

* test: add multi-bit numeric and bigint coverage for EncodedBitmap

Co-authored-by: Apollon77 <11976694+Apollon77@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert: remove accidental package-lock.json change

commit.message:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): add 5s context timeout to Forgejo commit search in bead detail

The bead detail handler called searchCommitsForBead with the raw request
context, allowing the parallel Forgejo API searches to run unbounded.
When Forgejo was slow, this caused bead detail pages to take 10-12
seconds, triggering E2EDeepTestFailing alerts.

Adding a 5-second context timeout bounds the total commit search time
and brings bead detail response time from ~12s to ~2s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix model init for masking-based ablation + add --channel_indices arg

Since BandSelect/ChannelSubset now use masking (zeroing) instead of
selection, tensor shape is always (T, 2, 16, freq). The model must
always use num_bands=2 and electrode_channels=16 regardless of
ablation config. Also added missing --channel_indices argparse arg.

commit.message:
fix: make golden test harness produce correct goldens (#156)

* fix: make golden test harness produce correct goldens

The golden test harness was broken — it captured Google Maps screenshots
instead of VanPilot because it used `am start` (which doesn't work for
Car App Library apps) and raw adb for APK install.

Changes:
- Replace `am start` with DHU command sequence (keycode home → tap icon)
  per docs/app-launch.md
- Add missing RPCs to InstanceManagerClient: dhu_command, install_apk,
  restart_dhu, adb_shell
- Use 50MB gRPC message limits (MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES from instance_manager)
- Apply crop_to_app_pane() to DHU screenshots (1920x1080 → 1832x1056)
- Replace raw subprocess adb install with IM install_apk RPC
- Add verify_vanpilot_foreground() check before capturing
- Add dhu_command() helper to GoldenTestCase for tab/mode switching
- Replace all TODOs in test_multi_state_golden.py with real DHU tap
  commands for tab switching and night on/off for dark mode
- Share instances across light-mode tab tests (golden-light-tabs)
- Remove stale subprocess import from harness
- Update unit tests for new client methods and message size options

Fixes #155

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: double-tap to launch VanPilot, improve foreground check

The DHU launcher requires two taps to launch an app: first selects/
highlights the icon, second confirms. Added second tap in
launch_vanpilot_via_dhu().

Car App Library apps run inside the Android Auto host process and may
not appear in `dumpsys activity activities`. Updated
verify_vanpilot_foreground() to also check `dumpsys activity services`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Agent <claude-agent@vanpilot.dev>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

DOT Physical card reads expiration from uploaded document

When medicalCardExpiration isn't set on the driver record, the DOT
Physical card now falls back to the expiration date from an uploaded

commit.message:
fix(http): add Cx cancellation support to connect_via_socks5

The SOCKS5 proxy connection function lacked a Cx parameter, making it
impossible to cancel during the multi-step handshake. Add cx: &Cx and
check_cx(cx)? calls before initial connect, after each I/O operation,
and after the final connect reply — consistent with the rest of HttpClient.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: establish stdio transport before HTTPS servers

The LocalMcpServerManager (Cowork mode) was failing with
"MCP error -32000: Connection closed" because when Claude Desktop
launches a second server instance for Cowork, the HTTPS servers
would crash on EADDRINUSE before the stdio transport was set up.

Fix: move stdio transport initialization BEFORE HTTPS servers,
and add EADDRINUSE error handlers so port conflicts don't crash
the process.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: heatmap BH tree walk now respects signal delay at leaf level

treePotential() was using instantaneous particle positions from the
quadtree, ignoring signal delay entirely. New particles appeared in the
potential field everywhere at once instead of propagating at c.

Now mirrors calculateForce() in forces.js: leaf nodes call
getDelayedState() per particle (skipping those outside the light cone),
while distant aggregate nodes still use current-time positions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add railway.toml to force Nixpacks builder (bypasses Railpack cache)

Railpack 0.17.2 auto-detection was overriding nixpacks.toml and using
a stale Docker layer cache. railway.toml with builder=NIXPACKS forces
Railway to use the Nixpacks builder which correctly reads nixpacks.toml:
- npm ci --include=dev (installs TypeScript)
- tsc --project tsconfig.json
- cp schema.sql to dist/

Also kept the postbuild script in package.json as belt-and-suspenders.
Added healthcheck config so Railway knows when the server is ready.

refactor(engine): extract tryClaimInvocation from claimWork (#90)

The three claim strategies in claimWork (affinity, unclaimed invocation,
direct entity claim) shared identical claim-with-rollback, affinity,
prompt-build, and emit-and-return logic. Extract into:

- tryClaimInvocation: claim invocation + rollback on race
- setAffinityIfNeeded: conditional affinity recording
- buildPrompt: enrich entity + build invocation
- emitAndReturn: emit entity.claimed + return ClaimWorkResult

Net: -37 lines, zero behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix tournament draft resume, prompt, and whitespace bugs (#952)

## Summary

- The season-1 draft crashed partway through (API timeout on DeepSeek at
pick 5, whitespace-only content from Opus on re-run) with no way to
resume — re-running required manually deleting the partial `draft` key
from the tournament JSON. Add resume support that detects a partial
draft and reconstructs pool/picks state to continue from the last
completed pick.
- Whitespace-only LLM responses (e.g. `'\n\n'` from Opus) passed the
`assert content` check because whitespace is truthy, then cascaded into
`parse_pick` which stripped it to `''` and failed confusingly. Extract
`_extract_content()` helper that strips content and falls back to
reasoning_content in one place.
- The draft system prompt didn't explain the draft format, so models
couldn't reason about pick position or strategy. Now includes
straight/linear draft order, seed position, number of players, and pool
visibility.
- Block `make tournament-draft` and direct `python ...
tournament_draft.py` execution in the enforcement hook since it costs
money.

## Test plan

- [x] `make check` passes (lint, typecheck, 994 tests)
- [x] Golden draft prompt files regenerated and verified
- [x] Hook correctly blocks `make tournament-draft` but allows `git add`
and `ruff format` on the file

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------
